guidelineMeta.tagline: Educational dashboard · Summaries are illustrative — verify against the published guideline
guidelineMeta.disclaimer: This dashboard does not provide medical advice. It organizes educational summaries aligned with guideline themes. Always use the official guideline PDF and local protocols for clinical decisions.

topics.title: ILD in CTD — background & burden
topics.summary: Why ILD matters in connective tissue disease: prevalence patterns, high-risk phenotypes, and the rationale for structured evaluation.

lenses.label: Clinical focus
lenses.body: Certain CTD populations carry disproportionate ILD risk; early recognition changes monitoring intensity and referral timing.

lenses.label: Concept map
lenses.body: Think of CTD–ILD as inflammation and fibrosis intersecting with autoimmunity — presentation can be insidious until function drops.

lenses.label: In practice
lenses.body: Document baseline symptoms, physiology, and imaging so change over time is interpretable, not just a single snapshot.

topics.pdfHint: Match to your PDF: Introduction / Background sections

topics.title: Screening & who to evaluate more closely
topics.summary: Frameworks for identifying patients who merit enhanced respiratory assessment beyond routine care.

lenses.label: Clinical focus
lenses.body: Screening is not one test — it combines phenotype, serology, symptoms, and objective measures when indicated.

lenses.label: Concept map
lenses.body: Risk stratification connects the immune diagnosis (which CTD?) to the lung phenotype (pattern and trajectory).

lenses.label: In practice
lenses.body: Agree what triggers escalation (e.g., new dyspnea, fall in DLCO, change on imaging) at your centre.

topics.pdfHint: Match to your PDF: screening algorithms / narrative questions on screening

topics.title: Diagnosis of ILD in the CTD context
topics.summary: Integrating history, physiology, imaging, and sometimes tissue diagnosis — with MDT input where available.

lenses.label: Clinical focus
lenses.body: The question is often not only ‘is there ILD?’ but ‘which pattern, how active, and how linked to the rheumatic disease?’

lenses.label: Concept map
lenses.body: Diagnosis is a Bayesian update: prior from CTD type + tests that discriminate mimic from overlap.

lenses.label: In practice
lenses.body: Spell out what was reviewed (PFT trends, HRCT reads, bronchoscopy/BAL if used) for the next clinician.

topics.pdfHint: Match to your PDF: diagnosis / MDT sections

topics.title: Treatment themes (immunomodulation & supportive care)
topics.summary: High-level themes: treating inflammation/fibrosis activity while managing comorbidity, vaccines, and supportive measures.

lenses.label: Clinical focus
lenses.body: Separate acute exacerbation, progressive fibrosis, and infection — management differs.

lenses.label: Concept map
lenses.body: Therapy balances rheumatic control with lung protection; toxicity monitoring is part of the treatment plan.

lenses.label: In practice
lenses.body: Pair drug plans with vaccination status, bone protection, and pulmonary rehab where appropriate.

topics.pdfHint: Match to your PDF: treatment / pharmacologic recommendations

topics.title: Monitoring, follow-up & when to reassess
topics.summary: Structured follow-up to detect progression early and align rheumatology–pulmonology intervals.

lenses.label: Clinical focus
lenses.body: Use consistent intervals for physiology and imaging so ‘noise’ is separated from real change.

lenses.label: Concept map
lenses.body: Monitoring is a closed loop: measure → interpret in context → adjust investigation or therapy.

lenses.label: In practice
lenses.body: Define who owns follow-up action items (who orders PFTs, who reads HRCT first) to avoid gaps.

topics.pdfHint: Match to your PDF: monitoring / follow-up recommendations

topics.title: Pregnancy, surgery, comorbidity intersections
topics.summary: Situations where multidisciplinary planning changes risk — not exhaustive, but a reminder to flag cross-specialty issues.

lenses.label: Clinical focus
lenses.body: Physiology, drug safety, and infection risk shift in pregnancy, perioperative states, and with age-related comorbidity.

lenses.label: Concept map
lenses.body: These are ‘context switches’: same ILD, different constraints on therapy and monitoring.

lenses.label: In practice
lenses.body: Document explicit handoffs between obstetrics, anaesthesia, ID, and primary teams when relevant.

topics.pdfHint: Match to your PDF: special populations / safety sections
